question: AD Cali vs. Llaneros FC: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Colombia Primera A game between AD Cali and Llaneros FC, scheduled for April 12 at 5:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if AD Cali and Llaneros FC combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on dimayor.com.co. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AD Cali vs. Llaneros FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for April 12 at 5:10 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for AD Cali vs. Llaneros FC tilts toward a competitive draw or narrow Llaneros edge, reflecting Llaneros' stronger recent form in Colombia's Categoría Primera B with three wins in their last five matches, including a 2-1 upset over a top-side. AD Cali, struggling with defensive injuries to key center-back Mauricio Cortés (confirmed out via official report) and midfielder inconsistencies, has lost two straight home games, eroding home advantage. Llaneros boast rest from a bye week and a solid head-to-head record (unbeaten in last three vs. AD Cali). Upcoming weather in Cali could favor Llaneros' counterattacking style, while AD Cali's must-win for playoff push adds pressure—watch pre-match lineups for shifts.

events.title: América FC vs. Grêmio Novorizontino - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 5:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: América-MG's dominant home form at Independência Stadium anchors trader consensus favoring them in this Série B clash against Grêmio Novorizontino, with recent odds reflecting a 65% implied probability of a home win amid the Minas Gerais side's four-match unbeaten streak. Promotion implications loom large for América, while Novorizontino prioritizes avoiding relegation; historical head-to-head tilts 3-1 to the hosts, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in current pricing.

events.title: Operário Ferroviário EC vs. Cuiabá EC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 12 at 5:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cuiabá enters the Copa do Brasil round-of-16 second leg at Operário Ferroviário's Estádio Germano Krüger with a 1-1 aggregate deadlock from the first leg, tilting trader consensus toward a low-scoring affair as Operário leverages strong home form (unbeaten in last five Série B home matches) against Cuiabá's shaky away record (winless in six Série A road games). Recent developments include no major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Cuiabá's fatigue from midweek Série A action contrasts Operário's rest advantage. Upcoming factors like Operário's defensive solidity (conceding under 1 goal per game at home) and Cuiabá's need for an away goal heighten upset potential, reflected in balanced market pricing emphasizing under 2.5 total goals.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Operário Ferroviário EC and Cuiabá EC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Upcoming factors like Operário's defensive solidity (conceding under 1 goal per game at home) and Cuiabá's need for an away goal heighten upset potential, reflected in balanced market pricing emphasizing under 2.5 total goals.

question: Based FDV above $75M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Based's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Based (https://x.com/BasedOneX) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Key recent development: forward Andrei Svechnikov's full return from offseason surgery strengthens Carolina's top-six forwards, while Flyers' goaltender Samuel Ersson remains day-to-day with a lower-body issue, forcing reliance on unproven backup Ivan Fedotov. Philly's road woes persist with a 0-2-0 away record and middling power play, contrasting the Canes' 5-1-0 start and rest advantage. Schedule density for Philadelphia ahead could amplify upset risk, but historical dominance favors Carolina 7-3 in last 10 meetings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nashville Predators enter their home matchup against the San Jose Sharks as heavy favorites, buoyed by an influx of scoring talent including Steven Stamkos and Jonathan Marchessault, who have combined for key goals in recent wins amid a 3-3-1 start. The Sharks, mired in a 1-5-1 slump, grapple with goaltending instability—Vitek Vanecek's .878 save percentage—and injuries sidelining William Eklund (upper body). Trader consensus reflects Predators' home-ice edge and superior firepower, with historical dominance (7-3 last 10 meetings) amplifying the implied 72% win probability. Watch for Sharks' rookie Macklin Celebrini spark, but Nashville's rest advantage post-off day tilts momentum firmly their way.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota Wild hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their Central Division matchup against the St. Louis Blues, with implied probabilities reflecting the Wild's hot streak of four wins in five games versus the Blues' three losses in four. Blues' home-ice edge at Enterprise Center tempers the line, but Wild's 3-1 record in last four head-to-heads underscores momentum heading into this early-season test.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their Western Conference Second Round matchup against the Colorado Avalanche, driven by Connor McDavid's explosive playoff form (12 points in 5 games) and home-ice advantage at Rogers Place. Colorado's elite power play (32.8% efficiency) counters Edmonton's stingy penalty kill, but Oilers' rest advantage post-first round could prove pivotal amid a grueling schedule. Historical head-to-heads favor Edmonton (6-4 last 10), amplifying momentum shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: LA Kings hold a clear edge over the Seattle Kraken in their Pacific Division clash, driven by LA's superior 21-11-3 record versus Seattle's middling 15-15-7 mark and recent three-game skid. Kings forward Adrian Kempe leads with 32 points, while Kraken's struggling power play (14.8% efficiency) hampers offense amid 3.4 goals against per game. No new injuries for LA's top line—Anze Kopitar and Quinton Byfield active—though Kraken's Brandon Montour remains sidelined (lower body). Home-ice at Crypto.com Arena favors Kings, who won both prior meetings this season, but Seattle's rest advantage after a day off tempers trader consensus at 65% LA win probability.

If the Jets win, the market will resolve to "Jets".
If the Golden Knights win, the market will resolve to "Golden Knights".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights enjoy a trader-favored edge in the matchup against the Winnipeg Jets, driven by home-ice advantage at T-Mobile Arena and a 5-1-0 start featuring potent offense from Mark Stone and Jack Eichel. Winnipeg counters with a 4-1-0 record and elite goaltending from Connor Hellebuyck, but faces challenges without injured defenseman Josh Morrissey, sidelined long-term with an upper-body issue. Recent Jets momentum includes a four-game win streak, yet Vegas boasts superior head-to-head history (4-1-1 last six) and rest advantage. Traders weigh these amid playoff-positioning implications in the Central Division, where upsets remain common early in the season.

question: Jets vs. Golden Knights: O/U 6.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 13 at 10:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Jets and Golden Knights combine to score 7 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 7, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

question: Will Israel launch a ground operation in Iran by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if participation by Israeli military personnel in a ground operation in Iran on or after February 28, 2026, is confirmed by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A "ground operation" is defined as a military operation that involves Israeli military ground forces conducting operations within Iranian territory.

For the purposes of this market, aerial and maritime operations will not be considered. Only operations involving Israeli troops on the ground within the terrestrial territory of Iran will qualify.

Official confirmation by the Israeli military of personnel operating on the ground within Iranian territory during the specified timeframe will resolve this market immediately. An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming that the Israeli military personnel operated on the ground within Iranian territory during the specified timeframe will also suffice.

Qualifying confirmations include statements such as the June 25, 2025, statement by IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir confirmed that Israeli commando forces operated covertly on the ground inside Iran during the 12-day war.

Confirmations referring only to non-military Israeli intelligence activity, including operations by the Mossad or Shin Bet, as well as airstrikes, cyberattacks, standoff weapons, remote sabotage, or actions by proxies or third parties without Israeli military presence, will not alone qualify.

Incidents that would not alone suffice include the November 2025 Mossad statement indicating its agents were present during protests in Iran, or the July 31, 2024, assassination of Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh in Tehran, widely attributed to Mossad agents.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information by the Israeli military; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily discounts any confirmed Israeli ground operation in Iran, pricing "No" outcomes near certainty amid a lack of official statements from Jerusalem, Tehran, or Washington. Recent escalations remain aerial: Israel's limited October 26 airstrikes targeted Iranian missile production sites in response to Tehran's October 1 barrage of 200 projectiles, with no verified ground incursions reported by primary military sources. Social media rumors of special forces persist but lack substantiation, reflecting historical patterns of shadow warfare over direct invasions. Upcoming Israeli security cabinet deliberations could signal further air actions, but a ground offensive into Iran would mark unprecedented escalation with high geopolitical risks.

question: Will Iran conduct a military action against Israel on March 19, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Israel’s soil on the listed date in Israel Time (GMT+2). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact Israeli ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Israel counts. Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip will not be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Israeli territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after the specified date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects low implied probabilities for Iranian military action against Israel, driven by de-escalation after Israel's limited October 26 airstrikes on Iranian defense sites, which Tehran downplayed as insignificant without retaliation. Iran's leadership signaled restraint, reserving a "legitimate" response amid U.S. calls for calm and a recent Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire reducing proxy pressures. Broader context includes Iran's nuclear advancements under IAEA scrutiny and Houthi disruptions, but no verified strike preparations. Upcoming catalysts like U.N. Security Council debates or Israeli operations in Syria could shift dynamics, though mutual deterrence and oil market stability favor inaction based on recent patterns.

Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Israeli territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Iranian ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after the specified date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have taken place.

question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $1,800 on March 24?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Tanzania vs Malawi
description:

question: Will the price of XRP be less than $1.10 on March 24?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Tanzania vs Malawi - Most Sixes Tanzania Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Tanzania and Malawi scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Malawi.The outcome corresponding to Malawi will be considered correct if Malawi is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tanzania.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Tanzania vs Malawi - Team Top Batter Tanzania Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Tanzania and Malawi scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Tanzania.
The outcome corresponding to Malawi will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Malawi.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even battle for top batter honors in the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash between Tanzania and Malawi, with both teams at 50% implied probability amid a viable 49.5% draw chance due to potential rain or low totals. Head-to-head history is sparse but competitive, with no batter dominating; neutral Entebbe pitch favors pace over spin, leveling attacking opportunities and keeping the race tight as both sides chase semifinal spots.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Saint Helena
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Eswatini and Saint Helena scheduled for March 24 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eswatini enters as heavy favorites in trader consensus for their T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash against Saint Helena, driven by superior squad depth and recent tournament form. Eswatini notched a convincing 104-run win over Seychelles earlier this week, showcasing balanced bowling led by Ringane Magagula's 3 wickets, while Saint Helena suffered a 168-run drubbing by Rwanda, exposing batting frailties averaging under 50. No head-to-head history exists, but Eswatini's higher ICC rankings and experience in regional qualifiers tilt probabilities sharply. Neutral venue in Uganda favors the more cohesive side; watch for dew impacting second innings chasing, though upsets remain possible in unpredictable associate cricket.
eventMetadata.league: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B
